IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript:
We will be removing the existing overhead electricity cables and replacing them with new underground electricity cables.
During the planned power outage, our contractors will remove two spans of the high-voltage electricity cables, including the associated poles and a transformer. In addition, we have identified 5 low-voltage electricity poles that require replacement and 2 new poles to be installed. These will be removed and new poles installed, along with a new three-phase transformer. Doing this will reduce the risk of you having an unplanned power cut in the future as we appreciate your area has been impacted with unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months. To complete the work safely we need to turn your power off. To ensure the power is off for the shortest possible time, our contractors Avidety may carry out preparation works prior to the power cut. This work is taking place along Forsten Lane.

In addition, we have identified 5 low-voltage electricity poles that require replacement and 2 new poles to be installed. These will be removed and new poles installed, along with a new three-phase transformer. Doing this will reduce the risk of you having an unplanned power cut in the future as we appreciate your area has been impacted with unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months. To complete the work safely we need to turn your power off. To ensure the power is off for the shortest possible time, our contractors Avidety may carry out preparation works prior to the power cut. This work is taking place along Forsten Lane.

Sent by Aaron at 19:36 - I'm pleased to confirm that all power in the Lancashire Road area should be back on. The issue was caused by an unexpected underground network fault, which our engineers diverted power around, while the repairs continue.

We know that a power cut can be difficult, and I'm sorry for the length of the time you were without your electricity. Please rest assured that our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep the lights on.

If your power isn't on or there's anything you'd like to talk to us about, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.

Sent by Aaron at 17:49 - Thanks for your patience over today.

Our engineers have let us know it's going to take longer than advised to excavate and repair the cabling. To give them time to do this safely, the estimated time frame to restore your power is now between 00:00 and 01:00.

We're sorry this is taking longer than expected, and we'll continue to keep you up to date, but if any change to this time comes in from our staff I'll let you know as soon as we hear.

Sent by Aaron at 16:10 - Thanks for bearing with us over today. Our team of cable jointers have arrived and have now identified the suspected section of faulted underground cabling using their specialist equipment.

Our digging team will now begin excavations, and this can take some time depending on the surface material, and how much space is needed to allow our engineers to assess the fault and make their repairs safely. 

Our estimated time frame of 17:00-18:00 doesn't need to change currently. We'll continue to keep you updated, but our team are always here if you have any questions.

Sent by Simon at 22:56 - We're very sorry we've had to switch off your electricity. Our engineers have turned off the power in the Edenbridge TN8 area in the interest of the public's health and safety. This is so they can investigate reports of an overhead cable that is on the ground.

If in the area, please take care and stay at least 10m away from the cable. 

Until our engineers arrive on site and investigate, it's difficult to advise you a time frame. However, based on an average for this kind of issue, our estimated time to have you back on is between 01:30 and 02:30. 

We'll keep you updated with our progress, but in the meantime our team are always here to talk on 105 if you have any questions or concerns.
